title: Writing - Part 1: Reading and Books
description: Your class has been discussing whether young people today read enough and the role of books in modern life.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the aspects in your notes. You should explain which aspect you consider most important, giving reasons for your opinion.

opinions: Reading for pleasure has declined significantly due to social media and video games.
Books develop critical thinking in a way that screens cannot.
It does not matter whether you read physical books or digital ones.

structure.introduction: Introduce the topic of reading and books and the two aspects you will discuss.
structure.bodyParagraph1: Discuss the first aspect (e.g., improving language and vocabulary) and its educational value.
structure.bodyParagraph2: Discuss the second aspect (e.g., developing imagination and empathy) and its personal benefits.
structure.conclusion: Evaluate both aspects and state which you consider most important.

content.modelAnswer:
In an age of smartphones, streaming services and social media, many people worry that the habit of reading books is in decline. Yet books continue to offer unique benefits that other media cannot easily replicate. This essay will consider how reading improves language skills and how it develops imagination and empathy.

Reading regularly is one of the most effective ways to expand vocabulary and improve writing ability. Exposure to well-crafted prose teaches readers how to express themselves clearly and precisely. Students who read widely tend to perform better academically across all subjects, not just in literature, because strong language skills support learning in every area.

Beyond language, fiction in particular develops the imagination and nurtures empathy. When readers follow characters through different experiences and perspectives, they gain a deeper understanding of emotions and human behaviour. This ability to put oneself in another's position is an invaluable social skill that benefits all relationships.

In conclusion, while both benefits are significant, I believe developing empathy through reading is ultimately the most important aspect. Language skills can be improved through various means, but the capacity for empathy and human understanding that good literature provides is truly unique and deeply valuable for society as a whole.
